Ntc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Pattison of Jackson Lewis (394.9400) for defendant and moving party; Danielle L. Tizol of the Nelson Law Group (650.794.2617) for plaintiff and responding party

DEFENDANT DAN MOTTAZ VIDEO PRODUCTIONS, LLC MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. The arbitration agreement lacks mutuality and is substantively unconscionable. Arbitration is mandated for discrimination, compensation, and physical injury claims that are commonly brought by employees. The arbitration agreement and employment agreement provides for in-court adjudication of claims that are most commonly brought by the employer-confidentiality, trade secrets, etc. The "cost sharing" provision is also unconscionable. Unconscionability permeates the agreements and the clauses will not be severed. See Armendirez v. Foundation Health (2000) 24 cal.4th 83. Request to present oral testimony denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
